degraded: External review (external_review.py — GPT-5.4 + Gemini 3.1 Pro via OpenRouter): the review diff was generated after origin/main advanced past the worktree base (the grade-cta-adopt PR merged mid-run), so GPT-5.4's 6 findings about plugins/shipwright-grade/scripts/lib/html_report.py + grade tests are ARTIFACTS of the stale base (that delta reversed in the diff), NOT this change — dismissed after verifying those files are untouched vs origin/main. GPT-5.4's one on-target finding (combine_coverage.py label parsing brittle to pytest-cov xdist worker suffixes) was FIXED (resolve_plugin_label longest-prefix + test). Gemini 3.1 Pro returned a null/empty feedback body (model-side) — no findings; coverage was preserved by the internal Opus code-reviewer, which cleared the core mechanic and raised 4 findings (1 medium partial-combine fail-open FIXED + tested; 3 low: scope documented, AC3 wording fixed, W4 dual-format heuristic left as pre-existing intentional contract).

surface_verification.justification: No user-facing web/CLI application surface — this iterate is framework CI + coverage instrumentation (a new combine tool, a tracked coverage.total, and the W4 verifier gate). Executable end-to-end proof was still run: the full combine->record->W4 chain executed against real suites (all 13 plugins + shared + integration, all green) producing a combined repo-wide 80.2% line-rate, which lit W4 SKIP->PASS on the committed files; plus 71 hermetic coverage tests. The surface_verification.py runner has no app to drive.

behaviors.behavior: AC1 — per-plugin --cov + per-tier COVERAGE_FILE in ci.yml (plugins with scripts/, shared, integration); preview (no scripts/) runs tests without coverage
AC2 — combine_coverage.py folds per-tier data into ONE repo-relative coverage.xml (per-plugin [paths] remap), absent-safe, idempotent, xdist-worker-suffix safe, partial-combine fails loud
AC3 — diff-cover runs over the combined coverage.xml AFTER all tiers; repo tier label via measure_diff_coverage DEFAULT_TIER=repo; step stays non-gating
AC4 — record_coverage_total.py writes tracked top-level coverage.total, preserves iterate_latest, atomic, refuses fabricated total on bad input
AC5 — W4 lit green: recorder total + calibrated coverage.min => PASS (was SKIP); round-trip + min-below-total on committed files
AC6 — coverage.diff stays transient (never mutates tracked results); grade byte-identical with/without coverage; dashboard tier label defaults to repo
AC7 — .gitignore covers .coverage* + .cov-data/ (per-tier data + combine dir never tracked)
AC8 — CI-gate guard stays green on the rewired ci.yml (no test dir silently uncovered; diff-cover allowlist entry intact)
behaviors.evidence: check_ci_gate_coverage.py --project-root . => OK (18 test dirs); enforced as the 'Run CI-gate guard' CI step
